subject: Ecology
title: Aldo Leopold
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Aldo Leopold.

hook.text: Aldo Leopold shot a wolf in 1909 and spent the rest of his life regretting it.

buildup.text: He watched the 'fierce green fire' die in her eyes and rethought predator control.

discovery.text: His 'land ethic' said humans are members of the ecosystem, not rulers over it.

twist.text: Leopold argued an action is right only if it preserves the integrity of the land.

climax.text: His book 'A Sand County Almanac' became the bible of conservation ecology.

punchline.text: A wolf's death taught him how ecosystems think.

quiz.question: What is Leopold's 'land ethic'?
options: Humans are members of the ecosystem, not rulers
Land should be used for maximum profit
Only scientists should manage wild areas
